item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Vetoing this, I'm afraid. While a dark take on the pure land is potentially interesting, I would rather have something that is more directly associated with Buddhism than with 093 (which, I feel, stands as its own thing, rather than a thing based on existing religions).

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: vetoed by author

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP mentions a "pure land", which I assume is some aspect of Buddhism. However, in this, I propose linking to SCP-093, considering that both involve a religious land of purity that is intrinsically tied to religion.

Editing this:

The target is thus transported to a dimension tentatively classified as SCP-2781-A. Based on first-person observation, SCP-2781-A is hypothesised to be a "pure land".

To this:

The target is thus transported to a dimension tentatively classified as SCP-2781-A. Based on first-person observation, SCP-2781-A is hypothesised to be a "pure land".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Triangular Dragon"

I see what you did there. Clever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i love the idea behind Blue text

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Took me a second try to figure out what was going on with the format screw, but I think this works. It tells a story in an interesting way, and that's all you can really ask for. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's exactly correct. The precedent is SCP-835 if I'm not mistaken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does pop more than another color would. I think it's a good style and I hope people keep using it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe the blue text is part of a mechanism to easily detect recent edits?

But I chose blue text since I always felt it to be representative of last edited text and SCP-2998 influenced me in that stylistic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The way it infects the document makes me think of a buddhist hatbot. Their text invasions are even the same color!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dislike how the boddhisatva editing the document acts as if he/she doesn't quite believe his own learnings in order to pretend to be part of the Foundation.

That's because the editor was formerly in the Foundation, hence why he decides to edit the SCP file. I suppose that when he got back to the stuff he knew from life, he adopted the stylistic of the Foundation.

'Hypothesized'? If the boddhisatvas aren't even certain of it themselves, why would they exert so much effort to attract people to the 'pure land'?

I will chalk that to that particular instance's (SCP-2781-969/Kata) insistence of scientific methodology. For now, it is only its word that indicate any existence of SCP-2781-A. The other instances are unlikely to confirm it (and the Foundation would not deem it to be strong alibi, unless they explore the place with people who come back to tell the tale).

So 'hypothesised' was a compromise to show that the 2781-969/Kata is aware of how the Foundation would tread on the matter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dislike how the boddhisatva editing the document acts as if he/she doesn't quite believe his own learnings in order to pretend to be part of the Foundation.

This is attributed to the Buddhist belief that humans are most likely to receive dharma effectively

Wouldn't 'understanding' be better than 'belief' in this case?

Based on first-person observation, SCP-2781-A is hypothesised to be a "pure land".2

'Hypothesized'? If the boddhisatvas aren't even certain of it themselves, why would they exert so much effort to attract people to the 'pure land'?

I know that this is for the purpose of maintaining the pretense of official Foundation documentation, but then that defeats the purpose of Trip editing the document in the first place to explain what the UMIBOZU actually are. Plus, I already know from the opening RAISA warning that the stuff in blue is vandalized text, so the pretense doesn't work for me.

item.title: Thanks, Attribution, and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
Firstly, allow me to express thanks to the man parties involved in the article's critique such as A Random Day, Roget, Soulless, Kid F, Stringfish, Onionbasher etc.

Image attributions are as follows:

  • Image 1 from Wikipedia (https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kuwana_-_The_sailor_Tokuso_and_the_sea_monster.jpg) under Public Domain
  • Image 2 from Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kanmon_Straits) under Public Domain

As for other matters,

+ show block
– hide block

SCP-2781-969 is the late Agent Kata, returned as a 'bodhisattva of the high seas'. To him, he is trying to relieve the suffering of the Foundation by helping them contain SCP-2781 as a whole as a Middle Way between his duties as a bodhisattva and a Foundation member.

And the other instances are essentially trapped at two points (Site-2781 and their respective original places) while being translucent. If they are translucent and not opaque, they cannot interfere with physical matter and cannot 'attack' people anymore.
